explanation: This command uses PowerShell to instantiate the WindowsInstaller COM object and install an MSI from a remote URL. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by including '-ComObject', 'InstallProduct', and a remote URL.

This command uses alternative syntax for creating the COM object using [activator]::CreateInstance and [type]::GetTypeFromProgID, which is equivalent to New-Object -ComObject. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.

This command uses pwsh (PowerShell Core) and includes the remote URL in the InstallProduct method. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by using '-ComObject' and 'InstallProduct' with a remote URL.
This command adds the '-WindowStyle Hidden' flag to obscure the PowerShell window, which is a common evasion technique. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses the '-C' shorthand for '-Command' and includes the remote URL in the InstallProduct method. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
